key_insight: TopoAgent replaces brittle linear multimodal reasoning with a self-evolving DAG of isolated atomic tasks, improving scientific problem solving through dynamic decomposition and runtime fission.
engineering_relevance: For fracture and damage workflows, this suggests a more robust way to structure complex multimodal analysis pipelines so each subproblem stays context-grounded and less prone to hallucination.
method: Front-end decomposer fractures complex queries into visually grounded atoms.
Atoms are arranged in a directed acyclic graph to enforce dependency-aware execution and strict context isolation.
Adaptive atomic fission splits bottleneck nodes into finer sub-atoms when tool limits are exceeded.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive evaluation across mathematics, physics, and chemistry benchmarks, with TopoAgent outperforming state-of-the-art linear agent frameworks.
watch_item: Performance may depend on correct graph decomposition and runtime splitting decisions, so implementation complexity and failure modes in atomization remain a practical concern.
paper_title: TopoAgent: A Self-Evolving Topological Agent for Multimodal Scientific Reasoning
